title: Planned Tree Removals at Lear Theater
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 2, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Matt Basile, Urban Forester
Matt Brezina, Parks Manager
DEPT: Parks and Recreation
SUBJECT: Planned Tree Removals at Lear Theater

This memo provides the notice and justification of the planned removal of two American Linden, Tilia americana, trees located in the parkway strip along Ralston Street adjacent to the Lear Theater at 528 W 1st Street. Removal of the two American Linden trees is necessary due to the two American Linden trees' declining conditions and increasing public safety risk. The two American Linden trees are the two largest American Linden trees in the City of Reno but are not currently recognized as state champions or designated as landmark trees.

Despite significant efforts by the Urban Forestry Division, including supplemental irrigation and multiple pruning attempts, the health of the two American Linden trees has continued to deteriorate. The two American Linden trees suffer from severe dieback, poor soil conditions, and inadequate irrigation, making further preservation of the two American Linden trees unsustainable. An official risk assessment by Lucas Proctor, a certified Tree Risk Assessor, confirmed the two American Linden trees' poor condition and recommended the two American Linden trees' removal to prevent potential safety hazards. The risk assessment identified a moderate risk of the two American Linden trees' branch failure, which could cause property damage to parked cars and personal injury to pedestrians. The moderate risk of branch failure, in addition to the two American Linden trees' declining health, makes removal the most responsible course of action to mitigate the risks of property damage and personal injury.

The two American Linden trees have been pruned by City arborists five times since 2000, and most recently in 2024, and there has been significant dieback since the most recent pruning in 2024. Pruning is no longer a sustainable or responsible option, and Matt Basile, Urban Forester, is recommending the two American Linden trees be removed.

Removal of the two American Linden trees is scheduled to take place by the end of April 2026.

fields.authors: Matt Basile, Urban Forester; Matt Brezina, Parks Manager
fields.tldr: The City of Reno's Urban Forestry Division is recommending the removal of two American Linden trees in the parkway strip along Ralston Street adjacent to the Lear Theater at 528 W 1st Street. Despite five rounds of pruning since 2000 and supplemental irrigation efforts, the trees have continued to decline due to severe dieback, poor soil conditions, and inadequate irrigation. A certified Tree Risk Assessor confirmed a moderate risk of branch failure, posing potential hazards to parked cars and pedestrians. Removal is scheduled to be completed by the end of April 2026.
fields.key_stats: Two American Linden (Tilia americana) trees; largest of their species in the City of Reno; pruned five times since 2000, most recently in 2024; moderate risk of branch failure identified; removal scheduled by end of April 2026.
fields.key_decisions: Approval of the planned removal of two American Linden trees located in the parkway strip along Ralston Street adjacent to the Lear Theater, based on declining health and public safety risk.
fields.action_items: Remove the two American Linden trees by the end of April 2026.

projects: Planned Tree Removals at Lear Theater
people: Lucas Proctor, certified Tree Risk Assessor
